pi_items.source: Magento 2 search weight specification (1-10 range); task requires setting SKU to maximum priority value. Adobe Commerce documentation confirms valid range is 1-10. Third-party guides recommend SKU=8-10 for optimal search prioritization.

Task specification and Magento 2 search weight range (1-10). Value is within valid range and represents mid-level priority. Adobe Commerce documentation confirms 1-10 is valid range. Third-party guides recommend Name=8-10, but task defines its own configuration.

Magento 2 search weight specification; matches default value (1) when no weight is specified. Valid range is 1-10. Adobe Commerce documentation: 'If no value is entered, all attributes default to a search weight of 1.' Task assigns description the default/minimum priority.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring Magento 2 product attribute search weights: SKU=10 (maximum priority), Product Name=5 (mid priority), Description=1 (minimum/default priority). All values fall within Magento's valid search weight range of 1-10, as documented in Adobe Commerce official documentation. The Magento environment contains synthetically generated test data (10 products, 4 categories, 3 customers) created via REST API during setup, running against a real MariaDB database. The search weight configuration affects product ranking in Magento's catalog search results.
